I’ve been using the Samsung Galaxy Buds Core for about a month now. My initial impression was quite positive, though I had some doubts about the 6mm driver—especially when compared to other earbuds offering larger 10–14mm drivers. I was also unsure about the claimed ~30–35 dB noise cancellation.However, after comparing them with the Realme Buds 7, which come with a 12mm driver, 52 dB ANC, LDAC, and Dolby support, the results genuinely surprised me. Despite having seemingly weaker specs on paper, the Samsung Buds Core actually performed better in real-world usage.Since I’m using a Samsung phone, the ecosystem integration is a big advantage. Features work smoothly, and the overall experience feels more refined and seamless.Sound Quality:The tuning is excellent. The bass, mids, and highs are well-balanced, and nothing feels overpowering. If needed, the equalizer allows further customization. Overall, the sound quality is impressive for its category.Noise Cancellation:Even though the ANC numbers are lower on paper, the real-world performance is quite effective and consistent.Microphone:I’d rate it around 4/5. Call quality is generally clear, but there are occasional voice breaks—possibly a software issue that could be improved with updates.Comfort & Fit:Initially, I experienced some discomfort, but after about a week, it improved significantly. The fit is secure and stable, though it may still feel slightly uncomfortable during longer usage for some users.Connectivity:The only downside is the lack of multipoint connection. However, switching between devices is still quick and seamless enough that it doesn’t feel like a major drawback.Final Thoughts:Despite modest specifications on paper, the Samsung Galaxy Buds Core deliver a surprisingly well-rounded experience. Strong tuning, good ecosystem support, and reliable performance make them a solid choice—especially for Samsung users.Read more

Review Title: Great premium earbuds, just not perfect across all setups.
Review Description: I recently picked up the Samsung Galaxy Buds3 Pro and after a couple of weeks of use here’s how they stack up.Pros: Top-tier sound for the price – the dual-driver + high-res codec combo gives surprising detail. The microphone/call quality is excellent even in noisy backgrounds as they fit my ears perfectly hence passive noise cancellation is good too. If you’re in the Samsung ecosystem you’ll appreciate the seamless device switching and app features and the case supports wireless charging too.Cons: Fit may not be ideal for smaller ears, and the ANC, while good, doesn’t completely block out all ambient noise compared to the very best (compared to Buds2 Pro). Some features only work fully with Samsung phones like (SSC), so if you’re using iPhone or a generic Android you won’t get full value. The stem controls (pinch/swipe) have a learning curve and sweaty workouts may make them finicky. Battery life is meh for prolonged use (4-4.5hrs with ANC and 5.5-6hrs w/o ANC)Summary: One less star just because of the battery backup. Review Title: Great Sound, Comfortable Fit, and Seamless Samsung Integration
Review Description: Samsung Galaxy Buds 4 Pro – Great Upgrade from Oppo Enco X2 (Especially for Samsung Users)I upgraded from the Oppo Enco X2, which were honestly fantastic and very underrated earbuds. Because of that, my expectations were pretty high. After using the Samsung Buds 4 Pro for a while, I can confidently say they feel like a solid upgrade for my use.First Impressions & DesignI really like the design and packaging. I chose the black color because I didn’t want the AirPods-like look that the metallic finish gives.I use a Samsung S23, and the pairing experience was completely seamless. No additional app installation was needed - all the earbud controls and settings are integrated directly into the phone settings. The ecosystem integration is definitely a big advantage if you use Samsung devices.Comfort & FitThe fit is excellent. I’ve worn them for long periods during the day and they remain comfortable with no ear fatigue. They’re lightweight and stay secure in the ears.Sound QualityThis is where the earbuds really shine.Out of the box the sound is good, but it becomes much better after tuning the EQ. I did the ear fitting test and experimented with the equalizer settings.I found Dynamic EQ or a custom EQ to work best.What stood out:- Clear instrument separation- Punchy and controlled bass- Customizable sound depending on your tasteI’ve been listening to a lot of techno lately, and these earbuds handle it really well.I’m also using UHQ codec support, which likely helps extract the best sound quality.Compared to my Enco X2:- Bass feels stronger- Instrument separation is clearer- Tonal balance can be easily adjusted with EQANC & Ambient ModeThe ANC is good - better than the Enco X2 in my experience, though maybe slightly behind AirPods.Ambient mode is also improved and sounds fairly natural. That said, these aren’t features I personally rely on a lot, so I didn’t test them extensively.Controls & Smart FeaturesOne feature I unexpectedly love is the voice commands and gesture controls.Being able to say things like:- “Play music”- “Stop music”- “Volume up / down”…and have the earbuds respond instantly is incredibly convenient.I also set up Gemini, which works seamlessly through the earbuds.Call QualityCall quality has been surprisingly good. Friends told me I sounded pretty normal during calls.So far I’ve only tested in quiet environments, but it already seems better than the Enco X2, which used to pick up a lot of surrounding noise.Other FeaturesThere’s also 360 audio, though personally I wasn’t a big fan when I tried it. It might work better for certain content like movies.The settings themselves aren’t overloaded with marketing terms, but there are enough customization options to make the earbuds fun to tweak.Pros✔ Excellent sound quality with EQ tuning✔ Comfortable and lightweight for long use✔ Seamless integration with Samsung phones✔ Useful voice commands and gesture controls✔ Good call qualityCons✖ Premium pricing✖ ANC may not be the absolute best compared to some competitors✖ 360 audio didn’t impress me muchFinal VerdictFor me, this is definitely an upgrade from the Oppo Enco X2.If you’re already in the Samsung ecosystem, these are an easy recommendation. The integration alone makes the experience much smoother.I was debating between Sony, Bose, and Samsung, but so far I have no regrets choosing Samsung.Read more

Review Title: Bought it for its battery back-up but got more
Review Description: Let me start with the elephant in the room claim - 24 hours battery back up from buds alone. I received this TWS around 6:45 in the evening, took about a couple  minutes to check the box contents and make sure the TWS was in perfect condition. Paired them with my phone only to find out the earbuds had 100% charge, so I started using them. I initiated my "testing" at 7 p.m. with The Jurassic Park (1993) at about 80% volume. 2 hours later, the buds had ~93% charge left. I said, "Challenge accepted!", and then watched the sequel, The Lost World (1997). Another 2 hours and some odd minutes later, the time was 11:10 p.m., the left bud showed 86% battery and the right was at 85%. At this point, I could see myself losing the battle, but I didn't wanna give up. I thought to myself, "Maybe if I tried harder, I'd win." But time, as always, wasn't on my side. It was 11:20 p.m., the silence of the night could be heard loudly and the fatigue of the day was catching up to me, when suddenly, an idea popped into my head. I rushed over to YouTube, searched for a 10 hour heavy metal music video, hit the play button, increased the earbuds volume to 100%, and then put both my phone and the buds on the bedside table grinning to myself thinking, "Yeah, survive that.", and called it a night. The morning came and as my vision cleared up to show 7:20 a.m. on the clock, I reached out to my phone, assuredly and confidently convinced, I had won the war.I didn't. The right bud was at 41% and the left at 45%. At this juncture, I gladly accepted my defeat and decided to write this story, a story of a brave TWS, a story that will never be in any history books, a story where the eternal TWS won against its sadistic owner.As for the rest, I will keep it brief;In-ear Detection: Works very well, no complaints.Sound Quality: Signature mode is too bass-y, not for me, for sure. Balance mode does the job and it does it well.Call quality: THE BEST. Tested against Boat Rockers 255 Neo, Boult Audio X50, Dizo GoPods Neo, Realme T100.Build Quality: Buds good. Case lid, when closed, has a slight wobble to it. Everything else is upto the mark.One final remark would be in-ear fitting, and thus, noise isolation. I find these buds to provide noise isolation as good as Dizo Gopods Neo with its 25Db ANC mode on. It almost feels like they were custom made for my ears. But do keep in mind that this can and will differ from person to person.Thank you for your time and I hope I didn't waste it.Read more

Review Title: The Ultimate Disappointment: A Scathing Review of the Worst Earbuds I’ve Ever Used
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.Let me preface this review by saying I’ve tested countless earbuds over the years, ranging from budget-friendly options to premium audiophile gear. Never in my life have I encountered a product as fundamentally flawed, poorly designed, and outright infuriating as these earbuds. If you’re considering purchasing these for gaming purposes, casual listening, or even as a backup pair—STOP. Save your money, your sanity, and your ears. From the abysmal mic functionality to the laughable sound quality, and the build quality that feels like it was engineered by someone who’s never held a pair of earbuds before, this product is a masterclass in failure. Let’s break down why these earbuds are an insult to the very concept of functionality, clarity, and performance.1. Mic Functionality: A Joke for Gaming PurposesLet’s start with the mic functionality, a feature that’s absolutely critical for gaming purposes or even casual Zoom calls. If you’re buying earbuds to communicate with teammates, stream, or participate in online meetings, prepare to be humiliated. The microphone picks up every conceivable noise except your voice. Background chatter, the faint hum of your refrigerator, even your dog barking three rooms away—all of it is crystal clear. But your actual voice? Muffled, distorted, and buried under a layer of static that makes you sound like you’re speaking through a tin can underwater.During a critical raid in my favorite MMO, my teammates repeatedly asked, “Are you chewing gravel? Why does your mic sound like a broken walkie-talkie?” When I switched to these earbuds’ mic functionality, the response was unanimous: “Never use these again.” For gaming purposes, where clear communication is non-negotiable, this mic is a liability. Even after tweaking settings, updating drivers, and screaming into the microphone like a madman, the clarity never improved. It’s almost impressive how a company could engineer a microphone this bad.2. Gaming Purpose? More Like “Game Over”Speaking of gaming purposes, these earbuds fail on every front. Latency is so severe that actions in-game—like gunfire or footsteps—are delayed by a full second, making competitive play impossible. Imagine hearing an enemy’s shotgun blast after you’ve already been respawned. The lack of clarity in spatial audio means you can’t distinguish between sounds coming from your left or right, a death sentence in shooters or horror games.But the real kicker? The performance of these earbuds under load is laughable. After just 30 minutes of use, they overheat to the point of discomfort, and the audio begins to crackle like a campfire. For a product marketed toward gaming purposes, the complete disregard for basic functionality is staggering.3. Functionality: A Textbook Example of “How Not to Design Earbuds”The functionality of these earbuds is so counterintuitive, it feels like a prank. The touch controls are hypersensitive yet unresponsive—a paradox that defies logic. Adjusting the volume? Prepare for a 50/50 chance of either muting the audio entirely or triggering your voice assistant. The companion app, which promises to “enhance your experience,” is a glitchy mess that crashes more often than a toddler’s toy car.Even basic functionality like pairing the earbuds to a device is a nightmare. They disconnect randomly, refuse to reconnect unless reset, and occasionally pair to the wrong device altogether (yes, my smart fridge now has audio privileges). For a product that claims to prioritize performance, the sheer lack of reliability is inexcusable.4. Clarity? More Like “Muddy Mess”If you value clarity in your audio experience, steer clear. The sound profile is so unbalanced it’s comical. Bass is overblown to the point of distortion, mids are nonexistent, and highs are shrill enough to shatter glass. Listening to music feels like being trapped in a nightclub with blown-out speakers—except even nightclubs have better sound quality.For podcasts or audiobooks, the lack of clarity turns every narrator into a garbled mess. Voices blend into the background noise, and subtle details are drowned out by the earbuds’ own hissing. Even at maximum volume, dialogue feels distant, as if the speaker is standing in another room. The sound quality isn’t just bad—it’s offensive.5. Performance: A Race to the BottomThe performance of these earbuds is a slap in the face to anyone who’s ever used a functional pair. The battery life, advertised as “up to 8 hours,” barely lasts two hours at 50% volume. And charging them? The case takes longer to power up than a medieval siege engine. Even the earbuds functionality during calls is a disaster. Every conversation becomes a game of “Guess What I Said,” with the other person constantly asking you to repeat yourself.Under stress—like multitasking between apps or running background downloads—the performance nosedives further. Audio stutters, the mic cuts out, and the earbuds emit a high-pitched whine that could double as a dog whistle. For a product that claims to prioritize performance, it’s clear no one tested these under real-world conditions.6. Earbuds Functionality: A Comedy of ErrorsThe earbuds functionality extends beyond poor sound and controls. The ergonomic design is a joke. They don’t fit securely in any ear shape, requiring constant adjustments mid-use. During a jog, one earbud flew out and nearly became roadkill. The “sweat-resistant” coating? A myth. After one gym session, the left earbud shorted out, emitting a smell akin to burnt plastic.Even simple tasks, like skipping a track or answering a call, require Herculean effort. The touch-sensitive areas are tiny and poorly mapped, leading to accidental hangs-ups or unintended playlist shuffles. For earbuds functionality that’s supposed to simplify your life, these add nothing but frustration.7. Sound Quality: An Assault on the SensesThe sound quality is where these earbuds truly cement their status as the worst on the market. The audio is flat, tinny, and devoid of depth—like listening to music through a cardboard tube. Genres that rely on nuance, like classical or jazz, are rendered unrecognizable. Rock and pop tracks lose their energy, with guitars sounding like kazoos and vocals like autotuned robots.Even for non-audiophiles, the sound quality is unbearable. Watching movies? Dialogue is drowned out by inconsistent volume levels, and action scenes lack punch. The lack of clarity and dynamic range makes every audio experience feel like a punishment.8. Build Quality: Cheap, Flimsy, and Downright DangerousThe build quality is a testament to corner-cutting. The plastic feels like it was salvaged from a dollar-store toy, with seams that creak under minimal pressure. The charging case’s lid wobbles like a loose tooth, and the earbuds themselves are so fragile, I’m afraid to breathe on them too hard.Within a week, the right earbud’s mesh grill fell off, exposing the speaker to dust and debris. The charging pins corroded after minimal use, and the case’s hinge now emits a death rattle every time it’s opened. For a product that costs more than a meal for two, the build quality is unforgivable.Conclusion: A Perfect Storm of FailureIn summary, these earbuds fail at every metric: mic functionality, gaming purposes, functionality, clarity, performance, earbuds functionality, sound quality, and build quality. They’re poorly designed, shoddily constructed, and utterly unfit for any use case. The sound quality is grating, the mic functionality is a joke, and the build quality is a safety hazard.If you value your time, money, and sanity, avoid these earbuds at all costs. For gaming purposes, communication, or even passive listening, there are countless better options—including earbuds half the price. These aren’t just bad; they’re an insult to the very idea of technology.Final Verdict: 0/10. The only thing these earbuds excel at is making you regret your purchase.Read more

Review Title: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)
Review Description: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)Okay, I need to rave about these! After seeing the hype (and that 5K+ bought last month stat!), I grabbed the boAt Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black, and honestly? They've exceeded every expectation. If you're looking for premium features without the insane price tag, stop scrolling – these are it.Here's why I'm obsessed:1.  Cinematic Spatial Audio is REAL: This isn't just marketing fluff. Watching movies or binge-watching shows is a whole new experience. The sound genuinely feels like it's coming from around you – immersive, deep, and incredibly engaging. Music benefits massively too, feeling richer and more layered than any earbuds I've owned before.2.  Battery Life That Defies Logic: 50 HOURS?! This is the absolute game-changer. The earbuds themselves last ages (around 10 hours on a single charge for me), but the charging case just keeps going. I charged the case fully over a week ago, used the buds daily for hours (commutes, workouts, calls), and I still haven't seen a low battery warning. It's liberating not to constantly worry about charging. MASSIVE win.3.  Crystal Clear Calls with 4-Mic AI ENx: Taking calls on these is a dream. I've tested them on busy streets, windy balconies, and even in a slightly noisy cafe – the person on the other end consistently says I sound clear and focused. The AI noise cancellation for the mics really works wonders. No more "Can you repeat that?" frustrations.4.  Fast Charge is a Lifesaver: Forgot to charge? 10 minutes in the case genuinely gives you hours (around 2-2.5 in my experience) of playback. Super convenient when you're rushing out.5.  boAt App = Awesome Customization: The app is simple but powerful. I love tweaking the EQ presets (bass boost for workouts, podcast mode for clarity) and customizing the touch controls. Finding the earbuds via the app is also a handy feature.6.  Rock-Solid Connection & Low Latency: Bluetooth 5.3 is flawless. Pairing was instant, and the connection stays strong even when my phone is in another room. Gaming and watching videos is seamless – absolutely zero noticeable lag or audio-video sync issues.7.  Comfort & Build: The Gunmetal Black looks and feels super premium. They fit snugly and comfortably in my ears, even during longer sessions or workouts. The IPX4 rating gives me peace of mind against sweat and light rain.8.  Great Sound Signature: Bass is punchy and satisfying without being overwhelming, mids are clear for vocals, and highs are crisp. Perfectly tuned for everything from EDM to podcasts.Minor Nitpick (because nothing's perfect):The touch controls are sensitive (great!), but there's a slight learning curve to avoid accidental pauses/skips when adjusting them. You get used to it quickly though.Verdict:The boAt Airdopes 300 deliver phenomenal value. You get flagship-level features – incredible 50H battery life, truly immersive spatial audio, excellent call quality with AI ENx, fast charging, app support, low latency, and a sleek design – all at a very reasonable price point. The hype is real, and the 5K+ purchases last month make total sense.If you're looking for reliable, feature-packed, great-sounding TWS earbuds that won't die on you halfway through the day, look no further. The Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black are an easy 5-star recommendation! boAt has absolutely knocked it out of the park with these. Highly impressed!Read more

Review Title: ⭐ Honest Review: Samsung Galaxy Buds Core (SM-R410)
Review Description: ⭐ Honest Review: Samsung Galaxy Buds Core (SM-R410)Sound Signature These earbuds are tuned for people who like soft, balanced, and clear audio. If you want loud, punchy sound with heavy bass, these are not for you.That said, on Samsung phones (especially Android 14+), sound output and overall experience are noticeably better.✅ ProsExcellent sound claritySoft, balanced, and clean audio. Vocals and instruments sound natural, not harsh.Outstanding call qualityMic quality is top-class for this segment. Calls are clear, crisp, and stable. No complaints here.Good ANC performanceANC works well for daily use like traffic, fans, and office noise. Not flagship-level, but effective.Super comfortable fitIn-ear fitting is excellent. Buds stay secure during walking, workouts, or daily movement. Very hard to drop.Solid build qualityFeels well made and durable. Case and buds both feel premium for the price.Very good battery lifeLong-lasting battery on both earbuds and case. Easily lasts a full day of use.Best value in Samsung ecosystemWith a Samsung phone, the experience is smooth and polished. Totally worth the money.❌ ConsNot loud or bass-heavyPeople who love loud volume and punchy bass may feel disappointed, especially on non-Samsung phones.Connectivity issues on non-Samsung devicesOccasional problems like:No auto-connect sometimesDelayed connectionPlay/pause malfunctionMic not working on calls occasionallyApp communication errorsANC doesn’t work with single earbudANC requires both earbuds to be in use.Samsung device required for full featuresTo unlock app features, you must first register the earbuds using a Samsung phone.Otherwise, they work as basic but decent earbuds.Wireless charging missingThis feature would have made it perfect.AI features not applicableNo practical AI usage despite Samsung branding.🔚 Final VerdictSamsung phone users:Close your eyes and buy it. No second thoughts. The experience is excellent.Non-Samsung users:Still recommended, but expect lower loudness and occasional connectivity hiccups. Once connected, performance is stable except for volume.Overall:Great earbuds. Amazing within Samsung’s ecosystem. Comfortable, reliable, and excellent for calls and balanced music. Review Title: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)
Review Description: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)Okay, I need to rave about these! After seeing the hype (and that 5K+ bought last month stat!), I grabbed the boAt Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black, and honestly? They've exceeded every expectation. If you're looking for premium features without the insane price tag, stop scrolling – these are it.Here's why I'm obsessed:1.  Cinematic Spatial Audio is REAL: This isn't just marketing fluff. Watching movies or binge-watching shows is a whole new experience. The sound genuinely feels like it's coming from around you – immersive, deep, and incredibly engaging. Music benefits massively too, feeling richer and more layered than any earbuds I've owned before.2.  Battery Life That Defies Logic: 50 HOURS?! This is the absolute game-changer. The earbuds themselves last ages (around 10 hours on a single charge for me), but the charging case just keeps going. I charged the case fully over a week ago, used the buds daily for hours (commutes, workouts, calls), and I still haven't seen a low battery warning. It's liberating not to constantly worry about charging. MASSIVE win.3.  Crystal Clear Calls with 4-Mic AI ENx: Taking calls on these is a dream. I've tested them on busy streets, windy balconies, and even in a slightly noisy cafe – the person on the other end consistently says I sound clear and focused. The AI noise cancellation for the mics really works wonders. No more "Can you repeat that?" frustrations.4.  Fast Charge is a Lifesaver: Forgot to charge? 10 minutes in the case genuinely gives you hours (around 2-2.5 in my experience) of playback. Super convenient when you're rushing out.5.  boAt App = Awesome Customization: The app is simple but powerful. I love tweaking the EQ presets (bass boost for workouts, podcast mode for clarity) and customizing the touch controls. Finding the earbuds via the app is also a handy feature.6. I’ve been using the OnePlus Buds 4 since September 24, 2025, and even after long-term daily use, they continue to perform exceptionally well.The highlight feature is Golden Sound once you complete the ear scan and setup, the audio becomes noticeably personalized and immersive. The sound feels fuller and more engaging, to the point where you can actually feel the music. What makes this feature even better is that it works across any device, not just OnePlus phones, which is a big advantage over many ecosystem-locked competitors.The Active Noise Cancellation is genuinely effective for everyday use, handling background noise well without feeling artificial. Battery life has also been reliable: the earbuds provide around 5–6 hours of continuous playback, and the charging case offers approximately 4–6 full recharges, making it easy to get through a full day or more without worry.The build quality is durable to a reasonable extent, though rough use especially with the black variant can lead to visible scratches. My unit is the black color, which looks very dignified, remains lightweight, and is easy to carry without feeling bulky. Despite being plastic, the buds feel far more premium than most in this segment; in hand, they could easily be mistaken for metal or another high-grade material.Overall, the OnePlus Buds 4 focus on delivering strong fundamentals sound quality, ANC, and dependable battery life and they do so consistently, making them a very satisfying long-term purchase.Read more

Review Title: Bought it for its battery back-up but got more
Review Description: Let me start with the elephant in the room claim - 24 hours battery back up from buds alone. I received this TWS around 6:45 in the evening, took about a couple  minutes to check the box contents and make sure the TWS was in perfect condition. Paired them with my phone only to find out the earbuds had 100% charge, so I started using them. I initiated my "testing" at 7 p.m. with The Jurassic Park (1993) at about 80% volume. 2 hours later, the buds had ~93% charge left. I said, "Challenge accepted!", and then watched the sequel, The Lost World (1997). Another 2 hours and some odd minutes later, the time was 11:10 p.m., the left bud showed 86% battery and the right was at 85%. At this point, I could see myself losing the battle, but I didn't wanna give up. I thought to myself, "Maybe if I tried harder, I'd win." But time, as always, wasn't on my side. It was 11:20 p.m., the silence of the night could be heard loudly and the fatigue of the day was catching up to me, when suddenly, an idea popped into my head. I rushed over to YouTube, searched for a 10 hour heavy metal music video, hit the play button, increased the earbuds volume to 100%, and then put both my phone and the buds on the bedside table grinning to myself thinking, "Yeah, survive that.", and called it a night. The morning came and as my vision cleared up to show 7:20 a.m. on the clock, I reached out to my phone, assuredly and confidently convinced, I had won the war.I didn't. The right bud was at 41% and the left at 45%. At this juncture, I gladly accepted my defeat and decided to write this story, a story of a brave TWS, a story that will never be in any history books, a story where the eternal TWS won against its sadistic owner.As for the rest, I will keep it brief;In-ear Detection: Works very well, no complaints.Sound Quality: Signature mode is too bass-y, not for me, for sure. Balance mode does the job and it does it well.Call quality: THE BEST. Tested against Boat Rockers 255 Neo, Boult Audio X50, Dizo GoPods Neo, Realme T100.Build Quality: Buds good. Case lid, when closed, has a slight wobble to it. Everything else is upto the mark.One final remark would be in-ear fitting, and thus, noise isolation. I find these buds to provide noise isolation as good as Dizo Gopods Neo with its 25Db ANC mode on. It almost feels like they were custom made for my ears. But do keep in mind that this can and will differ from person to person.Thank you for your time and I hope I didn't waste it.Read more

Review Title: The Ultimate Disappointment: A Scathing Review of the Worst Earbuds I’ve Ever Used
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.Let me preface this review by saying I’ve tested countless earbuds over the years, ranging from budget-friendly options to premium audiophile gear. Never in my life have I encountered a product as fundamentally flawed, poorly designed, and outright infuriating as these earbuds. If you’re considering purchasing these for gaming purposes, casual listening, or even as a backup pair—STOP. Save your money, your sanity, and your ears. From the abysmal mic functionality to the laughable sound quality, and the build quality that feels like it was engineered by someone who’s never held a pair of earbuds before, this product is a masterclass in failure. Let’s break down why these earbuds are an insult to the very concept of functionality, clarity, and performance.1. Mic Functionality: A Joke for Gaming PurposesLet’s start with the mic functionality, a feature that’s absolutely critical for gaming purposes or even casual Zoom calls. If you’re buying earbuds to communicate with teammates, stream, or participate in online meetings, prepare to be humiliated. The microphone picks up every conceivable noise except your voice. Background chatter, the faint hum of your refrigerator, even your dog barking three rooms away—all of it is crystal clear. But your actual voice? Muffled, distorted, and buried under a layer of static that makes you sound like you’re speaking through a tin can underwater.During a critical raid in my favorite MMO, my teammates repeatedly asked, “Are you chewing gravel? Why does your mic sound like a broken walkie-talkie?” When I switched to these earbuds’ mic functionality, the response was unanimous: “Never use these again.” For gaming purposes, where clear communication is non-negotiable, this mic is a liability. Even after tweaking settings, updating drivers, and screaming into the microphone like a madman, the clarity never improved. It’s almost impressive how a company could engineer a microphone this bad.2. Gaming Purpose? More Like “Game Over”Speaking of gaming purposes, these earbuds fail on every front. Latency is so severe that actions in-game—like gunfire or footsteps—are delayed by a full second, making competitive play impossible. Imagine hearing an enemy’s shotgun blast after you’ve already been respawned. The lack of clarity in spatial audio means you can’t distinguish between sounds coming from your left or right, a death sentence in shooters or horror games.But the real kicker? The performance of these earbuds under load is laughable. After just 30 minutes of use, they overheat to the point of discomfort, and the audio begins to crackle like a campfire. For a product marketed toward gaming purposes, the complete disregard for basic functionality is staggering.3. Functionality: A Textbook Example of “How Not to Design Earbuds”The functionality of these earbuds is so counterintuitive, it feels like a prank. The touch controls are hypersensitive yet unresponsive—a paradox that defies logic. Adjusting the volume? Prepare for a 50/50 chance of either muting the audio entirely or triggering your voice assistant. The companion app, which promises to “enhance your experience,” is a glitchy mess that crashes more often than a toddler’s toy car.Even basic functionality like pairing the earbuds to a device is a nightmare. They disconnect randomly, refuse to reconnect unless reset, and occasionally pair to the wrong device altogether (yes, my smart fridge now has audio privileges). For a product that claims to prioritize performance, the sheer lack of reliability is inexcusable.4. Clarity? More Like “Muddy Mess”If you value clarity in your audio experience, steer clear. The sound profile is so unbalanced it’s comical. Bass is overblown to the point of distortion, mids are nonexistent, and highs are shrill enough to shatter glass. Listening to music feels like being trapped in a nightclub with blown-out speakers—except even nightclubs have better sound quality.For podcasts or audiobooks, the lack of clarity turns every narrator into a garbled mess. Voices blend into the background noise, and subtle details are drowned out by the earbuds’ own hissing. Even at maximum volume, dialogue feels distant, as if the speaker is standing in another room. The sound quality isn’t just bad—it’s offensive.5. Performance: A Race to the BottomThe performance of these earbuds is a slap in the face to anyone who’s ever used a functional pair. The battery life, advertised as “up to 8 hours,” barely lasts two hours at 50% volume. And charging them? The case takes longer to power up than a medieval siege engine. Even the earbuds functionality during calls is a disaster. Every conversation becomes a game of “Guess What I Said,” with the other person constantly asking you to repeat yourself.Under stress—like multitasking between apps or running background downloads—the performance nosedives further. Audio stutters, the mic cuts out, and the earbuds emit a high-pitched whine that could double as a dog whistle. For a product that claims to prioritize performance, it’s clear no one tested these under real-world conditions.6. Earbuds Functionality: A Comedy of ErrorsThe earbuds functionality extends beyond poor sound and controls. The ergonomic design is a joke. They don’t fit securely in any ear shape, requiring constant adjustments mid-use. During a jog, one earbud flew out and nearly became roadkill. The “sweat-resistant” coating? A myth. After one gym session, the left earbud shorted out, emitting a smell akin to burnt plastic.Even simple tasks, like skipping a track or answering a call, require Herculean effort. The touch-sensitive areas are tiny and poorly mapped, leading to accidental hangs-ups or unintended playlist shuffles. For earbuds functionality that’s supposed to simplify your life, these add nothing but frustration.7. Sound Quality: An Assault on the SensesThe sound quality is where these earbuds truly cement their status as the worst on the market. The audio is flat, tinny, and devoid of depth—like listening to music through a cardboard tube. Genres that rely on nuance, like classical or jazz, are rendered unrecognizable. Rock and pop tracks lose their energy, with guitars sounding like kazoos and vocals like autotuned robots.Even for non-audiophiles, the sound quality is unbearable. Watching movies? Dialogue is drowned out by inconsistent volume levels, and action scenes lack punch. The lack of clarity and dynamic range makes every audio experience feel like a punishment.8. Build Quality: Cheap, Flimsy, and Downright DangerousThe build quality is a testament to corner-cutting. The plastic feels like it was salvaged from a dollar-store toy, with seams that creak under minimal pressure. The charging case’s lid wobbles like a loose tooth, and the earbuds themselves are so fragile, I’m afraid to breathe on them too hard.Within a week, the right earbud’s mesh grill fell off, exposing the speaker to dust and debris. The charging pins corroded after minimal use, and the case’s hinge now emits a death rattle every time it’s opened. For a product that costs more than a meal for two, the build quality is unforgivable.Conclusion: A Perfect Storm of FailureIn summary, these earbuds fail at every metric: mic functionality, gaming purposes, functionality, clarity, performance, earbuds functionality, sound quality, and build quality. They’re poorly designed, shoddily constructed, and utterly unfit for any use case. The sound quality is grating, the mic functionality is a joke, and the build quality is a safety hazard.If you value your time, money, and sanity, avoid these earbuds at all costs. For gaming purposes, communication, or even passive listening, there are countless better options—including earbuds half the price. These aren’t just bad; they’re an insult to the very idea of technology.Final Verdict: 0/10. The only thing these earbuds excel at is making you regret your purchase.Read more

If you prioritize comfort and prefer half-in-ear buds like I do, the Samsung Galaxy Buds 3 are a game-changer. I've always found traditional in-ear buds uncomfortable and struggled to find the perfect balance between comfort and sound quality. I previously used the Buds Live for their comfortable fit, but the sound didn't meet my expectations. I also have Soundcore in-ear models, which are good, but I couldn't wear them for extended periods.When Samsung announced the Galaxy Buds 3, I was eager to try them out, especially as I'm already embedded in the Samsung ecosystem. I prebooked them without hesitation, and I'm thrilled to say they’ve exceeded my expectations.The Galaxy Buds 3 are the best half-in-ear earbuds on the market. Their premium case and futuristic design fit snugly and comfortably in the ear, offering the feel of in-ear buds without the discomfort. Call quality is impressive, and listening to music is a treat with robust bass and well-balanced highs and lows. The active noise cancellation (ANC) is surprisingly effective, providing a quiet listening experience even in noisy environments.Features like AI voice commands work seamlessly, custom equalizer and 360-degree audio enhance the overall listening experience. The pinch & swipe gestures are intuitive and responsive, making it easy to control playback and manage calls. The neck stretch reminder is useful addition. Wireless charging is fast and compatible with other wireless and MagSafe chargers I use. Although it offers 2 to 3 fewer options compared to the Pro model in the Wear app, I find it perfectly adequate.In summary, if you're searching for comfortable half-in-ear earbuds with excellent sound quality and advanced features, the Samsung Galaxy Buds 3 are worth every penny. Look no further—these are truly fantastic!Read more

Review Title: Good call quality but stopped working in a mere 15 days. They replaced promptly.
Review Description: I initially wanted to buy the Buds 3 Pro but mistakenly purchased these in the hurry of Prime Day. However, I found the open design quite appealing. The sound quality is very good, not excellent, with noticeable ANC. The lows are loud, but the highs could use some improvement.The call quality is a significant improvement compared to all previous earbuds I've used, including the Buds 3 Pro and Sony WF-1000XM4.In terms of appearance, they look great, and I really love the case.Unfortunately, one earbud stopped working while I was at the gym. It wouldn't charge or be recognized by the Galaxy Wear app. I bought them on EMI, so now I'm stuck paying for something I can't use since parts aren't readily available at the service center. This kind of malfunction never happened with my previous earbuds, whether they were Realme, Samsung Buds 3 Pro, or Sony XM4. Because of this experience, I will refrain from buying new models on their release day from now on. I'm considering buying the new Nothing earbuds next time.Overall Ratings:Sound Quality: 4/5Appearance: 5/5Build Quality: 3/5 (one earbud stopped working)Comfort: 4/5 (open design allowed for longer wear without feeling air pressure, fit was great for me, never had issues doing jumping jacks or running. Needed to screw in sometimes to get the ANC right and align the sound opening to ear canals)After Sales Service: 4/5 (service centers are widespread in cities, but parts for this new model are not available)Update: they fixed and gave me back in 5 days.Read more

Review Title: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)
Review Description: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)Okay, I need to rave about these! After seeing the hype (and that 5K+ bought last month stat!), I grabbed the boAt Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black, and honestly? They've exceeded every expectation. If you're looking for premium features without the insane price tag, stop scrolling – these are it.Here's why I'm obsessed:1.  Cinematic Spatial Audio is REAL: This isn't just marketing fluff. Watching movies or binge-watching shows is a whole new experience. The sound genuinely feels like it's coming from around you – immersive, deep, and incredibly engaging. Music benefits massively too, feeling richer and more layered than any earbuds I've owned before.2.  Battery Life That Defies Logic: 50 HOURS?! This is the absolute game-changer. The earbuds themselves last ages (around 10 hours on a single charge for me), but the charging case just keeps going. I charged the case fully over a week ago, used the buds daily for hours (commutes, workouts, calls), and I still haven't seen a low battery warning. It's liberating not to constantly worry about charging. MASSIVE win.3.  Crystal Clear Calls with 4-Mic AI ENx: Taking calls on these is a dream. I've tested them on busy streets, windy balconies, and even in a slightly noisy cafe – the person on the other end consistently says I sound clear and focused. The AI noise cancellation for the mics really works wonders. No more "Can you repeat that?" frustrations.4.  Fast Charge is a Lifesaver: Forgot to charge? 10 minutes in the case genuinely gives you hours (around 2-2.5 in my experience) of playback. Super convenient when you're rushing out.5.  boAt App = Awesome Customization: The app is simple but powerful. I love tweaking the EQ presets (bass boost for workouts, podcast mode for clarity) and customizing the touch controls. Finding the earbuds via the app is also a handy feature.6.  Rock-Solid Connection & Low Latency: Bluetooth 5.3 is flawless. Pairing was instant, and the connection stays strong even when my phone is in another room. Gaming and watching videos is seamless – absolutely zero noticeable lag or audio-video sync issues.7.  Comfort & Build: The Gunmetal Black looks and feels super premium. They fit snugly and comfortably in my ears, even during longer sessions or workouts. The IPX4 rating gives me peace of mind against sweat and light rain.8.  Great Sound Signature: Bass is punchy and satisfying without being overwhelming, mids are clear for vocals, and highs are crisp. Perfectly tuned for everything from EDM to podcasts.Minor Nitpick (because nothing's perfect):The touch controls are sensitive (great!), but there's a slight learning curve to avoid accidental pauses/skips when adjusting them. You get used to it quickly though.Verdict:The boAt Airdopes 300 deliver phenomenal value. You get flagship-level features – incredible 50H battery life, truly immersive spatial audio, excellent call quality with AI ENx, fast charging, app support, low latency, and a sleek design – all at a very reasonable price point. The hype is real, and the 5K+ purchases last month make total sense.If you're looking for reliable, feature-packed, great-sounding TWS earbuds that won't die on you halfway through the day, look no further. The Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black are an easy 5-star recommendation! boAt has absolutely knocked it out of the park with these. Highly impressed!Read more
